learning_outcomes.description: Understand the theoretical background of, application of, and mathematical formulation for the following statistical procedures:1. z-test2. Repeated measures and independent groups t-tests3. Power4. ANOVA5. Correlation6. Multiple regression7. Non-parametric versions of each of the above
Write the 'Results' component of a research report.

Recognise assumptions and limitations in psychological research methods, including the underrepresentation of groups such as Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and its implications for the generalisability of research findings.

assessments.task_description:
• The mid-semester exam will be a 90 minute invigilated, open-book exam run by UQ.
  • The exam will assess theory and application of statistical concepts covered in lectures 1–4 and tutorials 1–3. Students will need to able to perform calculations covered in lectures and the tutorials to answer some questions.
  • The exam is open book so you can bring in any written and printed materials that you would like (e.g., lecture and tutorial slides; the student workbook, etc).
  • You will need a calculator for this exam. It must be a UQ approved calculator.
  • The exam will consist of multiple-choice questions only. Some multiple-choice questions will be stand-alone. Other multiple-choice questions will be components of one broader question.
  • The date for the mid-semester exam is set centrally by UQ. The exam will take place on a Saturday or Sunday.
  • Tutors will hold consultations for exam feedback after all students have completed the exam (times announced on Learn.UQ).
  • This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
• This assignment will be discussed in more detail during the semester.
  • Assignments are to be submitted electronically via Turnitin.
  • See information regarding extensions and late penalties within the School of Psychology.
  • Late assignments will not be accepted after marks have been released (approximately three weeks after assignments are submitted).
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance using an AI declaration form. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignments are to be submitted online via Learn.UQ/Turnitin.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
• The final exam will be an in-person, closed book exam held during the examination period and administered by the UQ Central Examinations unit.
  • The exam will assess theory and application of statistical concepts covered from Lecture 5 and Tutorial 4 onwards. Students will need to able to perform calculations covered in lectures and the tutorials to answer some questions. Students will also be required to interpret statistical results.
  • The exam will consist of multiple-choice questions only. Some multiple-choice questions will be stand-alone. Other multiple-choice questions will be components of one broader question.
  • This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 1

This lecture reviews material addressed in PSYC1040 such as normal distributions and z-scores.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture 1 This lecture reviews material addressed in PSYC1040 such as normal distributions and z-scores.

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 2

This lecture continues the revision of key concepts from PSYC1040 including: hypothesis-testing, single sample tests (when the population variance is known [z-test] and when the population variance is not known [single sample t test]).


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture 2 This lecture continues the revision of key concepts from PSYC1040 including: hypothesis-testing, single sample tests (when the population variance is known [z-test] and when the population variance is not known [single sample t test]).

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial 1

Fundamentals and single sample tests


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial 1 Fundamentals and single sample tests

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 3

This lecture covers two sample tests, using the same people twice (repeated measures t-test) and two different groups (independent groups t-test). We will also cover the assumptions of t-tests.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture 3 This lecture covers two sample tests, using the same people twice (repeated measures t-test) and two different groups (independent groups t-test). We will also cover the assumptions of t-tests.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial 2

Two sample t-tests


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial 2 Two sample t-tests

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 4

This lecture covers power and effect size.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture 4 This lecture covers power and effect size.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial 3

Statistical Power


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial 3 Statistical Power

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 5. ANOVA I

This lecture is the first of four lectures covering Analysis of Variance (ANOVA). An overview of one-way ANOVA will be presented, including the calculation of the omnibus test, partitioning of variance, and the underlying structural model.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture 5. ANOVA I This lecture is the first of four lectures covering Analysis of Variance (ANOVA). An overview of one-way ANOVA will be presented, including the calculation of the omnibus test, partitioning of variance, and the underlying structural model.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial 4

One-way ANOVA

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial 4 One-way ANOVA

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 6. ANOVA II

This lecture continues from the calculation of the omnibus tests of the one-way ANOVA to assess group differences using planned comparisons and post-hoc tests.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture 6. ANOVA II This lecture continues from the calculation of the omnibus tests of the one-way ANOVA to assess group differences using planned comparisons and post-hoc tests.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial 5

Follow-up tests for Independent-groups ANOVA

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial 5 Follow-up tests for Independent-groups ANOVA

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 7. ANOVA III

This is the third lecture on ANOVA and will cover how the analysis of variance can be used to test repeated time points.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture 7. ANOVA III This is the third lecture on ANOVA and will cover how the analysis of variance can be used to test repeated time points.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial 6

Repeated measures ANOVA

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial 6 Repeated measures ANOVA

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 8. ANOVA IV and Non-parametric tests I

This is the fourth lecture on ANOVA and begins a new topic on analyses that can be used to test data that do not meet the assumptions required to use t-tests or ANOVA.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Lecture 8. ANOVA IV and Non-parametric tests I
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture 8. ANOVA IV and Non-parametric tests I This is the fourth lecture on ANOVA and begins a new topic on analyses that can be used to test data that do not meet the assumptions required to use t-tests or ANOVA.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial 7

Factorial ANOVA & Non-Parametric Tests

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial 7 Factorial ANOVA & Non-Parametric Tests

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 10. Correlation I

This lecture introduces correlations, focusing on the calculation of r, variance accounted (r square), and non-parametric versions of r.

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture 11. Correlation II & Regression I

This lecture extends the concept of correlation (relationship between two variables) to regression (using a known association to make a prediction).

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

This final lecture further covers the concept of regression.
